nodes.label: Anti-Cheat Wave Validation System (Integration)
nodes.rationale: Validates gameplay server-side via upgrade rolling, frame-by-frame movement tracking, damage verification, and enemy death validation to prevent cheating

nodes.label: Wave Validation Anti-Cheat Flow
nodes.source_location: Wave Start / Completion / Upgrade Selection Flow
nodes.rationale: Three-phase validation: wave start issues 30s token + rolled upgrades; during wave frame data is sampled; wave complete submits telemetry for server-side multi-check validation with severity-tiered flagging

nodes.label: Dropped Upgrade Bundle System Design
nodes.rationale: Three invariants: (1) no mid-wave persistence — bundle effects apply immediately but commit to save only at wave end; (2) difficulty-agnostic via DifficultyManager; (3) stat floors from BASE_PLAYER_STATS to prevent curses from making stats worse than baseline

nodes.label: Curse System
nodes.source_location: Phase 1: Stat Minimums & Curse Data Layer
nodes.rationale: Negative stat modifiers found only in bundles (not purchasable). Use same UpgradeDefinition shape with isCurse:true. Stat floors enforced: maxHealth cannot go below BASE_PLAYER_STATS.maxHealth; attack damage cannot go below weapon base

nodes.label: DifficultyManager Singleton
nodes.rationale: Thin singleton wrapping the active Difficulty instance so all rarity lookups go through DifficultyManager.getCurrent() rather than importing NormalDifficulty directly, making the system difficulty-agnostic

nodes.label: BASE_PLAYER_STATS Constant
nodes.source_location: Phase 1: Stat Minimums & Curse Data Layer — Modified file: GameManager.ts
nodes.rationale: Exported constant from GameManager.ts holding the un-upgraded baseline stats (health:100, maxHealth:100, speed:200). Used as floors so curses cannot push stats below pre-upgrade baseline

nodes.label: Difficulty Interface
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for all wave-pacing data. WaveManager delegates to it for every wave-dependent decision. getRarityWeights(wave) is key for upgrade and bundle rarity rolling

nodes.label: GameManager Singleton
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for all runtime game state. Both Phaser scene systems and React components interact with it; React listens via EventBus events, Phaser systems call methods directly

nodes.label: EventBus
nodes.rationale: Typed pub/sub bus that decouples all systems. Used by anti-cheat (enemy-killed, damage-dealt), bundle system (spawn-bundle, bundle-picked-up), and upgrade system (upgrade-selected, upgrade-applied)

nodes.label: CollisionManager
nodes.rationale: Registers all arcade physics overlaps/colliders at construction. Emits EventBus events (damage-dealt, enemy-killed) for anti-cheat and spawns bundle drops after enemy kills

nodes.label: WaveValidationService
nodes.rationale: Frontend service that tracks frame samples, enemy deaths, and damage during a wave, then submits telemetry to /api/waves/complete for server-side validation

nodes.label: Attack Types (bullet, laser, zapper, flamer, spinner)
nodes.norm_label: attack types (bullet, laser, zapper, flamer, spinner)

nodes.label: WaveValidationToken (30-second cryptographic token)
nodes.rationale: Short-lived 30-second token issued at wave start and submitted at wave completion to prevent replay attacks and ensure the validated data corresponds to the actual wave play session

nodes.label: FlaggedWave (Suspicious Activity Record)
nodes.rationale: Multi-severity flagging (low/medium/high/critical). Critical flags trigger auto-ban. Stored in admin.flagged_waves for manual review. Categories: movement, damage, upgrades, kills

nodes.label: MainScene Frame Sampling Integration Pattern
nodes.rationale: Sample player position/velocity/health every 30 frames (~2x/sec at 60fps) via waveValidation.sampleFrame(). Use EventBus.on listeners for enemy-killed and damage-dealt rather than modifying EnemyManager directly

nodes.label: Item Selection at Collection Time Design
nodes.norm_label: item selection at collection time design

nodes.label: Frontend as Source of Truth Principle
nodes.norm_label: frontend as source of truth principle

links.source_location: Integration Points — UpgradeModal.tsx displays backend upgrades
What Needs To Be Done — Populate Upgrade Data from frontend JSON files
Relevant Codebase Context — GameManager.ts singleton game state
Phase 1 — Export BASE_PLAYER_STATS from GameManager.ts as stat floor source
Context — Bundles contain 1-4 upgrades and potentially 1-2 curses
Phase 0: DifficultyManager — all rarity lookups use DifficultyManager.getCurrent()
Steps 1-15 — structured implementation of bundle plan
Phase 1 — Curse stat minimums derived from BASE_PLAYER_STATS
Phase 2 — generateContents returns { upgrades, curses } via BundleContents interface
Phase 4 — bundleManager passed as 5th parameter to CollisionManager constructor
Phase 6 — subscribes to 'bundle-picked-up' via EventBus on mount
Phase 1 — Extract initial player stats from GameManager.ts into exported BASE_PLAYER_STATS constant
NormalDifficulty — constant object implementing the interface
Save CRUD — SaveManager persists game state from GameManager
GameHUD — displays health, wave, points, kills sourced from GameManager state
GameManager — updatePlayerStats emits player-stats-update; takeDamage emits player-death
handleProjectileEnemyCollision — emits damage-dealt; INTEGRATION_COMPLETE enemy-killed emit
Upgrade JSON Files — upgrades filtered by attack type; variant_upgrades.json per attack type
python-jose — used for JWT-based wave validation tokens and auth
Security Critical #1 — Hardcoded JWT Secret Key in config.py
Security Critical #2 — No MongoDB Authentication (MONGODB_URL without credentials)
